facts.context: Abraham Maslow proposed the idea that some motives are more imperative to survival than others. Which of the following approaches expresses this?
Which of the following statistics provides the most information about how spread out a distribution of scores is?
The AP Psychology examination given by the College Board in May exemplifies which of the following types of tests?
Michelle watches David Letterman on television, but doesn't recognize him when she walks past him in Manhattan. Which effect on perception does this best illustrate?
Abnormal behavior can be defined as maladaptive behavior according to
Which of the following responses was most likely acquired through classical conditioning?
facts.truth: The anxiety reaction of a woman who is driving on the highway for the first time after being involved in a major accident on that highway
distractors: The frightened cry of a baby who is disoriented upon waking up from a nap
The uncontrollable blinking of a contact lens wearer who has just gotten something in his eye
The startled cry of a child who has just been awakened in the middle of the night by a loud clap of thunder

facts.context: Which of the following scans can image brain function?

Turning up the volume on a music player changes which aspect of sound?
Which psychological perspective was most popular at the turn of the twentieth century in Western Europe?

One month before finals, Conrad makes a study schedule and begins to review his notes. Two weeks before finals, Conrad is studying for hours each day to prepare. Right after finals at the start of summer vacation, Conrad comes down with the flu. Which of the following theories best explains this chain of events?
Two "cognitive shortcuts" that can lead to errors in information processing are
facts.truth: the representativeness heuristic and the availability heuristic
distractors: prototypic development and fuzzy concept development

facts.context: A piano teacher is helping a student learn a new piece. At first, she praises the student for playing correct notes. After the student begins to show proficiency with the notes, the teacher only praises the student when he adds the proper dynamics and interpretation to the segments he plays. The teacher's method of instruction is an example of which of the following types of training techniques?
A group of participants in a sleep study are to be deprived of sleep for four days. After their second sleepless night, participants may begin reporting which of the following?
Iconic memory refers to
In a team tug of war, Ty did not pull as hard as he would have if he were pulling alone against one competitor. His behavior exemplifies

Which of the following most accurately explains why a pool with water temperature of 82 degrees may feel cool to a person who has been sunbathing, yet warm to a person who has been inside in the air conditioning?
Although Andy wanted to cut class to get to the Yankee opener, he came to class to take a quiz and review for an exam. According to Freud, this behavior evidences a strong
Physiological reactions to surprise or shocks are most controlled by which of the following parts of the nervous system?
A research study establishes that most people can taste one gram of salt in one quart of water. Which of the following concepts is most closely related to the goal of this study?
Standardized tests are
facts.truth: tests for which a person's performance can be compared with a pilot group
distractors: any examination given by your state or country
all examinations with exactly the same directions
tests with norms, which indicate average, high, and low scores for the test

facts.context: Which of the following is the best summary of Stanley Schacter's two-factor theory of emotion?
facts.truth: A combination of psychological changes and our cognitive interpretations combine to produce our emotional experiences.
distractors: An external event causes us to experience a specific emotion, and this emotion triggers certain physiological changes to occur.
When our body responds to an external event, our brain interprets the biological changes as a specific emotion.
Each person follows a predictable pattern of changes in response to stress, including alarm, resistance, and exhaustion.

distractors: primary symptoms of all mental disorders

facts.context: Implications of Harlow's study (of baby monkeys reared by artificial mothers) for humans include which of the following?
Which of the following is not a basic somatosensation?
Which of the following best explains why babies have poor vision for the first few weeks of life?
facts.truth: The neural connections to the primary visual cortex are not fully connected.
distractors: The nodes of Ranvier have not yet formed.
The axons are covered in tight coats of lipids, which impede neural firing.
The synaptic cleft of the neuron is filled with an aqueous humor.
